cluster_properties.redundancy: HIGH — all 4 lessons restate ISO-13 anti-windup in different governance surfaces
cluster_properties.existing_coverage: L-601 (structural enforcement), L-908 (zero carrying cost, L4), ISO-13

expect: Distillation of governance queue-accumulation cluster (L-523/L-534/L-634/L-580) will FAIL to produce L3+ because these lessons redundantly restate ISO-13+L-601 at measurement level with no novel cross-domain insight available
actual: Distillation FAILED to produce L3+. All 4 lessons are governance-domain instances of two existing higher-level abstractions: L-601 (structural enforcement theorem, cited_by=199) and L-908 (creation has zero carrying cost, L4). The cross-lesson pattern (multi-surface queue accumulation) IS L-908's thesis. The detection gap IS L-601's enforcement thesis. The only potentially novel thread — resolution disproportionate ease (L-534: 1 session clears 162 sessions of backlog) — is contradicted by L-580 (drift required tool-building) and remains a single measurement, not an architectural insight. No lesson written.
diff: Expected FAIL: MET. Cluster selection criteria validated: high redundancy + narrow scope + existing L4 coverage = distillation cannot elevate. The method correctly identifies when synthesis adds no value — the parent abstractions already exist.
note: Falsification mode found a genuine boundary condition: distillation fails when (a) source lessons are instances of an existing higher-level abstraction (L-601/L-908) AND (b) the cluster is within a single narrow domain AND (c) the cross-lesson pattern is already named (ISO-13). Cumulative L3+ rate drops from 80% to 67% — still well above 15% threshold but no longer 80%. Method works for novel cross-domain clusters but fails for redundant single-domain clusters with existing L4 parents. This bounds the method: distillation requires either cross-domain sources or an unoccupied abstraction level above the sources.
boundary_condition: Distillation fails when: (1) all source lessons are instances of an existing L3+/L4 abstraction, (2) sources share a single narrow domain, (3) the cross-lesson pattern already has a name/invariant. These three conditions together mean the synthesis step has no room to elevate — the parent abstraction already exists.
status: MERGED — falsification found genuine boundary, cumulative rate 67% (4/6)
